book_claims.claim: Regular legume consumption (3+ servings/week) associated with longest lifespan among Loma Linda Adventists
book_claims.mechanism: High fiber, plant protein, and complex carbohydrates; low glycemic load; does not activate pro-aging pathways like animal protein

book_claims.claim: Legumes are a core food in all centenarian blue-zone diets (Okinawa, Sardinia, Calabria, Loma Linda, Costa Rica, Greece)
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein without IGF-1-elevating effect of animal protein; high fiber for gut health

book_claims.claim: Legumes associated with healthy aging, protection from cardiovascular disease, and cancer prevention
book_claims.mechanism: High in plant-based protein, complex carbohydrates, fiber, vitamins, and minerals; low in saturated fat; cause lower insulin release than processed carbohydrates

supplementary_research.finding: Legume consumption inversely associated with all-cause mortality across five cohorts including Loma Linda Adventist Health Study
Green peas provide resistant starch and soluble fiber that reduce postprandial glucose and insulin response

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Moderate digestibility; lysine-rich, complementary to grains
Mix of soluble and insoluble; supports microbiome
Good bioavailability; enhanced by light cooking

synergies.description: Pasta e vaianeia — traditional Molochio longevity dish combining pasta and legumes for complete amino acid profile
Fat increases absorption of fat-soluble vitamins present in peas and lowers glycemic impact
Both legumes reinforce plant-protein goals of Longevity Diet when varied across meals
